description.p: Tesseract is a privilege-separated, post-quantum disk-encryption tool for the Linux desktop. A memory-locked, sandboxed background agent owns all key material and performs every cryptographic operation; the GTK4 GUI never touches a key.

ul.li: Encrypted file containers, whole partitions, and hidden volumes for plausible deniability
Cipher cascades (AES, Serpent, Twofish, Camellia, ChaCha20) up to depth 5
Post-quantum hybrid keyslots (X25519 + ML-KEM-1024) and attestation (ML-DSA-87)
Standalone file encryption to recipients (HPKE / RFC 9180), multi-recipient and signable
FIDO2 / YubiKey enrollment via the hmac-secret extension
Auto-dismount on lock, suspend, logout, idle, and a one-press panic that wipes keys
Fully non-root by default (FUSE + udisks2); optional dm-crypt fast path behind polkit
Modern, themeable UI: Dracula, Catppuccin (4 flavors), Vintage Light, Neon Tessera, and follow-system, with a live accent picker
